title: CrossMatrixConfiguration
description: ADR-0004 CONFIGURATION layer: the reusable, SYNCABLE cross-reference METHODOLOGY ("how things are glued") — scales, relation types + relation DECLARATIONS, and contraction chains. Holds NO members (Dimensions layer) and NO cells/observations (State layer). Aspects (House-of-Quality, JTBD, UX views-of-quality, ...) are INSTANCES of this one schema (ADR-0002: configure the axes, don't fork the engine), tagged by `aspect`. References the entity set by stable id+version (`dimensionSetRef`), and relation `from`/`to` are dimension ids — NEVER a $ref. Merged with a Dimensions doc + a State doc (model.open) to form the engine model.

configId.description: Stable logical identifier for this Configuration (the sync key, with `version`). NOT a file path.

version.description: Version of this Configuration. State binds an EXACT (configId, version).

aspect.description: The methodology family this configuration expresses — e.g. `hoq` | `jtbd` | `ux-views-of-quality`. Open (a new aspect is a new Configuration instance, not a new schema).

dimensionSetRef.description: Stable id+version reference to the Dimensions doc this Configuration's relations are declared over (NOT a $ref). The mcp resolves it from the repo library.

scales.description: Declared observation->value tables (the ONLY place tokens become numbers). Unipolar (values >= 0).

relations.description: Relation DECLARATIONS (no cells): which dimensions relate, the valence (relationType), scale, and coverage policy. Cells live in the State layer keyed by `id`.

RelationType.description: Supporting AND opposing valences as distinct types (separation, not a signed scale).

RelationDecl.description: A relation DECLARATION (no cells). `from`/`to` are dimension ids in the referenced Dimensions doc. `from == to` expresses intra-axis correlation (self-relation).
